name: Bar-b-q Brisket
recipeInstructions:
1. Ask your butcher for this particular cut of meat rather than using the fattier and much bigger packer trim brisket.
2. Generously season the brisket with the Head Country Seasoning. Leave the fat cap intact and season it as well.
3. Let marinate a minimum of one hour or overnight.
4. Build a fire with good quality charcoal and well-seasoned pecan wood.
5. Let burn at least 30 minutes to burn off the lighter fluid fumes and start a good bed of coals.
6. Place brisket away from direct heat and cook for 3 hours at 250 degrees Fahrenheit.
7. After three hours, wrap brisket securely in heavy aluminum foil.
8. Baste with Head Country Bar-B-Q Sauce.
9. Continue cooking for an additional 3 hours at approximately 325 to 350 degrees Fahrenheit until the thermometer registers an internal temperature of 180 degrees Fahrenheit.
10. To serve, remove the fat cap and slice across the grain for the tenderest portions.
11. Serve with Head Country Bar-B-Q Sauce.
